pick.rationale: Miami enters with a clear analytical advantage, ranking 30th in KenPom and 35th in Torvik compared to Missouri's 52nd and 46th, while boasting superior efficiency on both ends of the floor (34th in offense, 45th in defense versus Missouri's 50th and 68th). The Hurricanes' 25-8 record and ACC semifinal appearance—including a win over Louisville—demonstrates consistent quality, while Missouri's 20-12 mark and second-round SEC tournament exit to Kentucky reveals a team that struggled to close games against elite competition. The 1.5-point spread reflects a competitive matchup, and Missouri's SEC-tested roster with Mark Mitchell (32 points vs. Kentucky) gives them upset potential, but Miami's balanced attack led by NABC All-Region honorees Malik Reneau and Tre Donaldson should prevail in a close game.

result.text: Kentucky 78-72 Missouri (Mar 12, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Missouri Tigers vs Kentucky Wildcats SEC Tournament - 2nd Round Kentucky Wildcats Kentucky UK 21-12 , 10-8 SEC 78 21-12 , 10-8 SEC Final 1 2 T Kentucky UK 38 40 78 Missouri MIZ 29 43 72 Missouri Tigers Missouri MIZ 20-12 , 10-8 SEC 72 20-12 , 10-8 SEC Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats Videos Denzel Aberdeen's late basket pushes Kentucky past Missouri 78-72 at the SEC Tournament By AP Updated: Mar 12, 2026, 03:16 pm Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email NASHVILLE, Tenn. -- — Otega Oweh scored 21 points, Denzel Aberdeen added 16, including a key basket with 22.5 seconds remaining , and No. 9 seed Kentucky beat No. 8 seed Missouri 78-72 on Thursday in the second round of the SEC Tournament. Kentucky (21-12) advances to play No. 1 seed and fourth-ranked Florida in the quarterfinals on Friday. Kentucky led by 16 points with 14:33 left in the second half. But the Wildcats went nearly five minutes without a field goal as Missouri used a 10-2 run to get within 60-57 at the 8-minute mark. Missouri's second-half rally was led by Mark Mitchell , who scored 23 of his 32 points after the break. His basket with 2:34 left gave the Tigers their first lead of the second half at 70-69, but they did not make another field goal the rest of the game. After Kentucky forced an airball on a baseline jumper, Oweh drove into the lane at the other end for a 73-70 lead with 1:13 left. Then Oweh forced a turnover when he knocked the ball off the leg of Mitchell. Aberdeen received a pass with the shot clock winding down and he used a pump fake to get into the lane before banking in a contested shot for a 75-70 lead. He added two free throws in the closing seconds to seal it. Missouri (20-12) also got 13 points, seven rebounds and seven assists from T.O. Barrett . Up next Kentucky lost both regular-season meetings with Florida — a 92-83 contest on the road and 84-77 at home. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Kentucky (21-12) advances to play No. 1 seed and fourth-ranked Florida in the quarterfinals on Friday. Kentucky led by 16 points with 14:33 left in the second half. But the Wildcats went nearly five minutes without a field goal as Missouri used a 10-2 run to get within 60-57 at the 8-minute mark. Missouri's second-half rally was led by Mark Mitchell , who scored 23 of his 32 points after the break. His basket with 2:34 left gave the Tigers their first lead of the second half at 70-69, but they did not make another field goal the rest of the game. After Kentucky forced an airball on a baseline jumper, Oweh drove into the lane at the other end for a 73-70 lead with 1:13 left. Then Oweh forced a turnover when he knocked the ball off the leg of Mitchell. Aberdeen received a pass with the shot clock winding down and he used a pump fake to get into the lane before banking in a contested shot for a 75-70 lead. He added two free throws in the closing seconds to seal it. Missouri (20-12) also got 13 points, seven rebounds and seven assists from T.O. Barrett .
